title: Agent Loop
existing_short_definition: The core execution cycle of an AI agent — observe state, decide on an action, execute a tool, incorporate the result, repeat.

title: Agent Memory
existing_short_definition: Mechanisms that let AI agents retain information across turns or sessions — conversation buffers, summary memories, vector stores, scratchpads.

title: AI Agent
existing_short_definition: An AI that can take actions in the world — not just answer questions, but use tools, browse the web, write files, and complete multi-step tasks.

title: License (AI Model Licenses)
existing_short_definition: The legal terms governing how a model's weights can be used — ranging from permissive (Apache 2.0, MIT) to restricted (Llama Community License, research-only) to custom.

title: Artificial Intelligence
existing_short_definition: The broad field of building systems that perform tasks requiring human-like intelligence — perception, reasoning, language, decision-making.

title: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR)
existing_short_definition: Transcribing speech to text — dominated by OpenAI Whisper and specialized commercial providers like Deepgram and AssemblyAI.

title: Autonomous Agent
existing_short_definition: An AI system that pursues a goal with minimal human intervention, planning and executing multi-step work across tools and time.

title: Backpropagation
existing_short_definition: The algorithm that efficiently computes gradients of the loss with respect to every parameter by applying the chain rule backward through the network.

title: Benchmark
existing_short_definition: A standardized test used to measure and compare AI model performance.

title: Catastrophic Forgetting
existing_short_definition: The tendency of neural networks to lose previously learned capabilities when trained on new data, especially during fine-tuning.

title: Chain of Density
existing_short_definition: A summarization prompting technique that iteratively adds key entities to a summary while keeping word count constant, producing dense, information-rich summaries.

title: Classification
existing_short_definition: A supervised ML task where the model predicts a discrete class label from a fixed set of categories.

title: Code Interpreter
existing_short_definition: An LLM tool that writes and executes Python (or other) code in a sandbox, then uses the results — for calculation, data analysis, and reliable reasoning.

title: Browser Use / Computer Use
existing_short_definition: An LLM capability where the model controls a computer GUI — clicks, types, scrolls — via screenshot input and mouse/keyboard output, enabling browser and OS automation.

title: Context Engineering
existing_short_definition: The practice of deliberately shaping what ends up in an LLM's context window — retrieval, memory, tool output, and system prompt — to maximize task success.

title: Continual Learning
existing_short_definition: The research direction of training models on a stream of new data over time without catastrophic forgetting or needing to retrain from scratch.

title: Copilot Pattern
existing_short_definition: A human-in-the-loop UX where the AI suggests, edits, and assists in real time but the user retains control and approves every action.

title: Data Augmentation
existing_short_definition: Techniques for expanding a training dataset by generating modified copies of existing examples without changing their labels.

title: Dataset
existing_short_definition: A structured collection of examples — text, images, audio, tables — organized for training, evaluating, or benchmarking models.

title: Deep Learning
existing_short_definition: A subfield of ML that uses neural networks with many layers to learn hierarchical representations from raw data.

title: Double Descent
existing_short_definition: A phenomenon where test error decreases, then increases (classical overfitting), then decreases again as model capacity or training time grows past a threshold.

title: Embedding
existing_short_definition: An embedding is a list of numbers that represents the meaning of a piece of text — similar texts have similar numbers.

title: Emergence (Emergent Abilities)
existing_short_definition: The phenomenon where certain capabilities appear abruptly in LLMs as model scale crosses a threshold, rather than improving smoothly.

title: Extended Thinking (Claude)
existing_short_definition: Anthropic's reasoning mode where Claude produces long internal reasoning before its final answer, toggleable per request with a thinking-token budget.

title: Feature
existing_short_definition: An individual input variable a model uses to make predictions.

title: Feature Engineering
existing_short_definition: The craft of transforming raw data into features that expose the structure a model needs — dominant in tabular ML, largely replaced by learned features in deep learning.

title: Framework (AI)
existing_short_definition: A library or toolkit that handles the complex plumbing of AI applications so you can focus on your actual product.

title: Function Calling
existing_short_definition: A feature that lets an AI model request to run a specific function or tool instead of just generating text.

title: Generalization
existing_short_definition: A model's ability to perform well on data it has not seen during training.

title: Gradient Descent
existing_short_definition: The optimization algorithm that trains nearly every ML model by iteratively adjusting parameters in the direction that reduces loss.
existing_code_example:
# Vanilla SGD step
for x, y in loader:
    loss = model(x, y)
    loss.backward()
    for p in model.parameters():
        p.data -= lr * p.grad
        p.grad.zero_()

title: Grokking
existing_short_definition: A training phenomenon where a model overfits for a long time and then suddenly generalizes — a dramatic phase transition in generalization.

title: Human-in-the-Loop
existing_short_definition: A system design where human judgment is required for specific steps — labeling, approval, escalation — rather than full automation.

title: In-Context Learning
existing_short_definition: The ability of LLMs to perform a new task from a few examples given in the prompt, without any weight updates.

title: Inference Time Scaling
existing_short_definition: The empirical observation that LLM performance on reasoning tasks continues to improve predictably as you spend more tokens or compute at inference.

title: Instruction Tuning
existing_short_definition: Fine-tuning a base LLM on (instruction, response) pairs so it reliably follows user requests instead of just completing text.

title: Label
existing_short_definition: The ground-truth output associated with a training example in supervised learning.

title: Loss Function
existing_short_definition: A scalar metric that quantifies how wrong a model's predictions are; training minimizes it.

title: Machine Learning
existing_short_definition: A field of AI where systems learn patterns from data instead of being explicitly programmed with rules.

title: Translation (Machine Translation)
existing_short_definition: The task of converting text or speech from one language to another, preserving meaning.

title: Meta-Prompting
existing_short_definition: Using an LLM to improve or generate prompts for other LLMs — iteratively refining based on eval feedback or rubric scoring.

title: Modality
existing_short_definition: A single channel of information a model consumes or produces — text, image, audio, video, code, structured data.

title: Model Card
existing_short_definition: A document that describes an AI model — its capabilities, limitations, training data, intended uses, and license.

title: Multi-Agent System
existing_short_definition: Multiple AI agents working together, each with a specialized role, to complete complex tasks.

title: Multimodal
existing_short_definition: Models or systems that jointly process multiple input modalities — text, image, audio, video, code — in a single forward pass.

title: Named Entity Recognition
existing_short_definition: An NLP task that identifies and classifies spans of text into categories like person, organization, location, date, or money.

title: Neural Network
existing_short_definition: A stack of matrix multiplications and nonlinear activation functions that approximate arbitrary functions given enough data.
existing_code_example:
# A minimal 2-layer network in PyTorch
import torch.nn as nn
net = nn.Sequential(
    nn.Linear(784, 256), nn.ReLU(),
    nn.Linear(256, 10)
)

title: Open Source vs Open Weight
existing_short_definition: Open weight means the model's parameters are public. Open source means the code AND training data are also public.

title: Open Weights vs Open Source
existing_short_definition: The distinction between 'open weights' (model files released, often under usage restrictions) and 'open source' (training code, data, and process also available).

title: Orchestration (Agent Orchestration)
existing_short_definition: The control layer that decides what step an AI system takes next — which model, which tool, which branch, and when to stop.

title: Overfitting
existing_short_definition: When a model memorizes training data instead of learning generalizable patterns, so it performs worse on new inputs.

title: Post-Training
existing_short_definition: Everything done to a base model after pretraining — SFT, DPO/RLHF, safety tuning — to turn it into a useful assistant.

title: Pretraining
existing_short_definition: The first and most expensive stage of LLM training — learning general language patterns from trillions of raw tokens with next-token prediction.

title: Prompt
existing_short_definition: A prompt is the instruction or question you type to an AI — it's how you tell the AI what you want it to do.

title: Prompt Chaining
existing_short_definition: Decomposing a task into a sequence of simpler LLM calls where each output feeds the next.

title: Prompt Engineering
existing_short_definition: The practice of designing input text to LLMs to maximize desired output quality — including role setting, instructions, examples, and output format.

title: Question Answering
existing_short_definition: The task of producing an answer to a natural-language question, either from a passage, a knowledge base, or world knowledge.

title: ReAct Pattern
existing_short_definition: A prompting pattern that interleaves Reasoning steps with Action steps (tool calls), letting an LLM iteratively gather info before answering.

title: Reasoning (LLM Reasoning)
existing_short_definition: A model's ability to solve multi-step problems — math, logic, planning — by thinking through intermediate steps rather than pattern-matching to an answer.

title: Reflection (Self-Reflection)
existing_short_definition: A technique where an LLM critiques its own output and revises it, often improving quality at the cost of extra inference calls.

title: Regression (ML Task)
existing_short_definition: A supervised ML task where the model predicts a continuous numeric value rather than a discrete class.

title: Reinforcement Learning
existing_short_definition: A paradigm where an agent learns by trial and error, maximizing cumulative reward from an environment.

title: Scaling Laws
existing_short_definition: Empirical power-law relationships between LLM loss and three inputs: parameters, training tokens, and compute.

title: Self-Consistency
existing_short_definition: A reasoning trick where the model samples multiple chains of thought at nonzero temperature and picks the answer that appears most often.
existing_code_example:
answers = []
for _ in range(10):
    resp = llm.complete(prompt, temperature=0.7)
    answers.append(extract_answer(resp))
final = Counter(answers).most_common(1)[0][0]

title: Sentiment Analysis
existing_short_definition: Classifying text by emotional tone — typically positive, negative, neutral — or by more fine-grained emotions or aspects.

title: Diarization (Speaker Diarization)
existing_short_definition: Determining 'who spoke when' in a recording — clustering speech segments by speaker identity without knowing speakers in advance.

title: Summarization
existing_short_definition: The task of producing a shorter version of a document that preserves the key information.

title: Supervised Learning
existing_short_definition: Training a model on input-output pairs so it learns to predict the output given a new input.

title: System Prompt
existing_short_definition: Hidden instructions given to an AI before the conversation starts that shape its behavior and persona.

title: Test Data
existing_short_definition: A held-out dataset used exactly once at the end of training to estimate a model's real-world performance.

title: Test-Time Compute
existing_short_definition: The paradigm of spending more compute at inference time — via longer chains of thought, best-of-N, tree search — to improve performance on hard tasks without retraining.

title: Text Generation
existing_short_definition: The task of producing natural-language output from a model, one token at a time, conditioned on a prompt or context.

title: Text-to-SQL
existing_short_definition: Translating natural-language questions into SQL queries against a known database schema — one of the oldest practical LLM use cases.

title: Training Data
existing_short_definition: The examples used to update a model's parameters during learning.

title: Transfer Learning
existing_short_definition: Using a model trained on one task as a starting point for a different but related task instead of training from scratch.

title: Tree of Thought
existing_short_definition: A reasoning strategy where the model explores a tree of intermediate thoughts, evaluating and backtracking, rather than following a single linear chain.

title: Text-to-Speech (TTS)
existing_short_definition: Synthesizing natural-sounding speech from text — modern models include ElevenLabs, OpenAI TTS, Cartesia Sonic, XTTS, and open-source F5-TTS.

title: Underfitting
existing_short_definition: When a model is too simple to capture the structure of the data, so both training and validation performance are poor.

title: Unsupervised Learning
existing_short_definition: Training on data with no labels — the model discovers structure, clusters, or a representation on its own.

title: Validation Data
existing_short_definition: A held-out subset of data used during training to tune hyperparameters and detect overfitting — never used for gradient updates.

title: Vision Language Model
existing_short_definition: A multimodal model that understands both images and text in a single architecture, typically by projecting image features into the LLM's embedding space.

title: Voice Activity Detection (VAD)
existing_short_definition: Detecting when speech is present in an audio stream — the trigger that tells voice agents when to start/stop listening.

title: Voice Agent
existing_short_definition: An AI system that takes voice input and produces voice output in real time, often end-to-end through a multimodal LLM or via ASR → LLM → TTS pipeline.
